query: Ilya Sutskever Safe Superintelligence CEO June 2025 Daniel Gross left Meta buyout rejected source

{"query":"Ilya Sutskever Safe Superintelligence CEO June 2025 Daniel Gross left Meta buyout rejected source","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1775,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.cnbc.com/2025/07/03/ilya-sutskever-is-ceo-of-safe-superintelligence-after-meta-hired-gross.html","title":"Ilya Sutskever is CEO of Safe Superintelligence after Meta hired Gross","content":"- Ilya Sutskever on Thursday said he will assume the CEO role at Safe Superintelligence, the artificial intelligence startup he co-founded.
Sutskever’s announcement on Thursday comes after Meta poached Daniel Gross, a longtime entrepreneur and AI investor who had been CEO of Safe Superintelligence.
Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg has been on a multibillion-dollar AI hiring spree, highlighted by a $14 billion investment in Scale AI that brought the startup’s founder Alexandr Wang and a small group of his lead engineers to Meta.
Safe Superintelligence was reportedly valued at $32 billion in a fundraising round in April.
Choose CNBC as your preferred source on Google and never miss a moment from the most trusted name in business news."},{"url":"https://observer.com/2025/07/ilya-sutskever-safe-superintelligence-ceo-daniel-gross-meta/","title":"Ilya Sutskever to Lead Safe Superintelligence After Meta Snags CEO | Observer","content":"Zuckerberg recently unveiled its new unit, Meta Superintelligence Labs (MSL), which will be co-led by Scale AI founder Alexandr Wang and ex-Github CEO Nat Friedman.
The team includes talent recruited from OpenAI, Google DeepMind and Anthropic.
Friedman, also Gross’s investment partner at the venture capital firm NFDG, has previously backed SSI.
Zuckerberg initially attempted to acquire SSI outright and tried—and failed—to recruit Sutskever, according to CNBC.
As OpenAI’s chief scientist, he was a central figure in the failed attempt to oust CEO Sam Altman in 2023, and he left the company the following year to co-found SSI."},{"url":"https://techcrunch.com/2025/07/03/ilya-sutskever-will-lead-safe-superintelligence-following-his-ceos-exit/","title":"Ilya Sutskever will lead Safe Superintelligence following his CEO's exit | TechCrunch","content":"The announcement follows weeks of reporting that Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg was in advanced talks to hire Gross, as well as his longtime investing partner, former GitHub CEO Nat Friedman.
As you know, Daniel Gross’s time with us has been winding down, and as of June 29 he is officially no longer a part of SSI.
Sutskever started Safe Superintelligence shortly after leaving OpenAI, where he played a role in the brief ousting of CEO Sam Altman.
Zuckerberg has also nabbed some top researchers from OpenAI and Google DeepMind to fill out his new AI team.
Savings end June 26, 11:59 p.m."},{"url":"https://www.calcalistech.com/ctechnews/article/aqneiunfk","title":"“We we know what to do”: Ilya Sutskever takes helm as CEO, rules out sale of Safe Sup | Ctech","content":"Sutskever confirms previous CEO Daniel Gross is leaving the secretive AI startup and pledges to keep it independent.
Ilya Sutskever, co-founder of Safe Superintelligence (SSI), has confirmed that Daniel Gross, one of the startup’s co-founders and its CEO, officially left the company on June 29.
Founded just last year, SSI has attracted significant backing, including funding from Google, Nvidia, and leading venture firms, to pursue what it describes as safe superintelligent AI.
Nat Friedman, the former GitHub CEO and Gross' investment partner, will “partner with Alex” to lead Meta’s push on advanced AI products and applied research."},{"url":"https://mlq.ai/news/ilya-sutskever-steps-in-as-ceo-of-safe-superintelligence-after-daniel-gross-departs-amid-meta-poaching-attempts/","title":"Ilya Sutskever Steps In as CEO of Safe Superintelligence After Daniel Gross Departs Amid Meta Poaching Attempts | MLQ News","content":"Ilya Sutskever, co-founder of OpenAI, has taken over as CEO of Safe Superintelligence (SSI) following the departure of Daniel Gross, who left for Meta Platforms after a failed acquisition attempt."}]}

Ilya Sutskever will step in as CEO of a startup he co-founded last year. Ilya Sutskever, who co-founded Safe Superintelligence (SSI) last year, is stepping into the CEO role after the startup’s former leader, Daniel Gross, left for Meta amid an aggressive recruitment campaign. “We are grateful for his early contributions to the company and wish him well in his next endeavor,” Sutskever said. “The company’s future is very bright, and I expect miracles to follow,” he said in a post on X. In his new role at Meta, Gross will reportedly be working on A.I. products for MSL. As OpenAI’s chief scientist, he was a central figure in the failed attempt to oust CEO Sam Altman in 2023, and he left the company the following year to co-found SSI.","score":0.8488849,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.cnbc.com/2025/06/19/meta-tried-to-buy-safe-superintelligence-hired-ceo-daniel-gross.html","title":"Meta tried to buy Safe Superintelligence, hired CEO Daniel Gross","content":"# Meta tried to buy Ilya Sutskever's $32 billion AI startup, but is now planning to hire its CEO. * Meta recently tried to acquire Safe Superintelligence, the AI startup launched by OpenAI co-founder Ilya Sutskever, according to sources familiar with the matter. * When Sutskever rebuffed the offer, Mark Zuckerberg moved to recruit the startup's CEO and co-founder Daniel Gross instead. * Meta now plans to hire Gross and former GitHub CEO Nat Friedman, and take a stake in their venture fund, to beef up the company's AI team. When Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg poached Scale AI founder Alexandr Wang last week as part of a $14.3 billion investment in the artificial intelligence startup, he was apparently just getting started. The company's mission is to focus on safely developing a superintelligence, a computer-based agent capable of surpassing human intelligence. On May 15, 2024, OpenAI co-founder Ilya Sutskever left OpenAI after a board dispute where he voted to fire Sam Altman amid concerns about communication and trust. On June 19, 2024, Sutskever posted on X that he was starting SSI Inc, with the goal to safely develop superintelligent AI, alongside Daniel Levy, and Daniel Gross. In July 2025, co-founder Gross left the company to join Meta Superintelligence Labs, and Sutskever became the CEO of SSI. 1. ↑ \"Exclusive: OpenAI co-founder Sutskever's new safety-focused AI startup SSI raises $1 billion\". \"OpenAI's former chief scientist is starting a new AI company\".","score":0.80844593,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.techzine.eu/news/applications/132402/meta-snatches-ssi-ceo-after-failed-takeover-and-openai-staff","title":"Meta snatches SSI CEO after failed takeover and OpenAI staff - Techzine Global","content":"Techzine » News » Applications » **Meta snatches SSI CEO after failed takeover and OpenAI staff**. # Meta snatches SSI CEO after failed takeover and OpenAI staff. This AI startup was founded by OpenAI co-founder Ilya Sutskever.**. When Sutskever rejected the takeover proposal, Mark Zuckerberg (photo) tried to recruit the company’s CEO and co-founder, Daniel Gross, instead. It also wants to take a stake in their venture capital fund, with the aim of strengthening Meta’s AI team. CEO Mark Zuckerberg already brought in Scale AI founder Alexandr Wang last week as part of a $14.3 billion investment in that AI startup. Zuckerberg is now apparently turning his attention to Daniel Gross, the CEO of Safe Superintelligence, and Nat Friedman, according to people familiar with the plans. ## Safe Superintelligence rejected takeover. Sutskever, who founded the company just a year ago after leaving OpenAI, rejected both the takeover attempt and an offer to join Meta, the sources said.","score":0.7969415,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.83,"request_id":"9849fef4-395c-42fd-a2f5-91017f0f5698"}
